selectionRule: Resume the active checkpoint first; otherwise choose the highest-priority ready item owned by the running role whose dependencies are completed. Never bundle unrelated product items.

items.title: Complete verified Cloudflare baseline browser audit
items.risk: closed by exact-SHA GitHub Actions evidence
acceptance: record exact main SHA and matching immutable deployment receipt
record real Cloudflare URL and passed HTTP smoke test
inspect desktop and touch/mobile full-scroll behavior
record console, page-error and failed-request observations
verify reduced-motion behavior
exercise WebGL context loss and restoration

items.title: Self-host and audit active fonts
items.expectedGain: removes fragile font hotlinks and closes a documented provenance blocker without diluting the spectacle-first visual direction
items.risk: font metric drift, non-reproducible conversion or an incomplete fallback test

evidence.provenanceStatus: Inter and DM Mono authorship and SIL OFL 1.1 records verified
evidence.remainingBlocker: Google Fonts hotlink remains active; local WOFF2 candidate and blocked-font evidence are not yet shipped

items.nextAction: Verify exact current main SHA receipts, acquire the primary lease, vendor Inter variable upright plus DM Mono 400/500 with retained OFL records, replace only font-loading CSS, run install/check/build, and push iter-3 candidate.
acceptance: exact Inter and DM Mono sources, authors and licenses recorded
no runtime request to fonts.googleapis.com or fonts.gstatic.com
only the selected upright Inter and DM Mono 400/500 browser files are shipped
desktop, mobile and reduced-motion exact-SHA audits pass
title wrapping, panel alignment and mobile overflow do not materially regress
controlled local-font failure leaves the page readable and does not block WebGL, scrolling or navigation
items.abandonCondition: Abandon the selected file/conversion route only if it cannot produce traceable browser-supported WOFF2 or causes a critical typography/navigation regression that cannot be corrected within the typography-only scope; file size alone is not an abandon condition.

items.title: Replace linear camera descent with authored camera rail
items.expectedGain: major improvement to spatial storytelling, scene reveals and top-tier immersive-site pacing
items.risk: scroll desynchronization, discontinuity or motion sickness

evidence.source: current product direction and independent visual-review backlog
evidence.requiredBeforeStart: Q-006 completed or deliberately reprioritized by new visual evidence

items.nextAction: After the active item closes, inspect exact-SHA desktop screenshots and author distinct position/look-target keyframes per scene before changing geometry or assets.
acceptance: distinct camera position and look target per scene
continuous interpolation without visible jumps
desktop journey gains stronger reveals and spatial contrast
mobile and reduced-motion behavior remain usable
items.abandonCondition: Abandon a specific rail design if it repeatedly causes scroll discontinuity or motion sickness and a revised authored path does not resolve it within three evidence-backed strategies.

items.title: Create distinct procedural world identities
items.expectedGain: reduces repeated-cluster appearance and creates a memorable multi-scene visual progression
items.risk: visual incoherence or unstable rendering rather than ordinary GPU cost

evidence.source: current visual direction and research backlog
evidence.requiredBeforeStart: current active item completed and critic evidence confirms world repetition is the next highest gap

items.nextAction: Prototype one coherent batch with at least three scene-specific procedural systems and shared crystalline art direction.
acceptance: at least three visibly distinct procedural systems
shared art direction remains coherent
desktop presentation is materially more memorable
mobile remains usable and WebGL remains stable
items.abandonCondition: Abandon an individual geometry system if it reads as a disconnected style or causes reproducible instability; high but stable GPU cost alone is not an abandon condition.

items.title: Evaluate a CC0 snowy HDRI lighting pass
items.expectedGain: may add richer crystalline reflections and roughness depth
items.risk: literal photographic reflections or unstable loading; asset size alone is not a blocker

evidence.candidateStatus: researched only; no HDRI has entered a product candidate

items.nextAction: When assigned by main, select and vendor a licensed high-quality HDR/EXR variant, test environment lighting only, record transfer/decoded memory, and retain procedural-light fallback.
acceptance: licensed source, author, exact file and project path recorded
visible improvement in crystalline material depth
desktop and mobile browser audits remain passed
WebGL recovery and navigation remain intact
failure cleanly restores procedural lighting
items.abandonCondition: Abandon a specific HDRI if reflections read as a literal landscape, the effect is not clearly superior, or reproducible memory instability occurs; do not abandon solely because the asset exceeds a conservative size target.

items.title: Add cinematic loading and failure recovery
items.expectedGain: improves first impression and makes heavy spectacle assets feel intentional rather than broken
items.risk: fake progress, content trap or recovery state that obscures the experience

evidence.browserBaseline: context loss/restoration passed for the verified baseline
evidence.openGap: user-facing WebGL creation failure and cinematic initialization are not implemented

items.nextAction: Design a staged cinematic reveal tied to real initialization boundaries and a readable non-WebGL fallback without reducing the desktop effects budget.
acceptance: honest initialization progress or staged reveal
user-facing WebGL creation failure fallback
no content trap for reduced motion or mobile
desktop first impression materially improves
items.abandonCondition: Abandon a specific loader concept if it delays usable content without delivering a clearly stronger first impression or creates an inaccessible trap.

items.title: Implement measured adaptive quality
items.expectedGain: preserves ambitious desktop effects while preventing reproducible instability on weaker devices
items.risk: quality oscillation or conservative tiers that flatten the desktop experience

evidence.currentPolicy: performance is a blocker only for breakage, obvious instability or severe usability failure
evidence.requiredBeforeStart: direct evidence of instability or a heavy-effects batch requiring bounded fallback

items.nextAction: Only after direct instability evidence, define bounded effect tiers that preserve maximum desktop quality and degrade particles/post-processing before core art direction.
acceptance: desktop retains the highest visual tier
quality changes are bounded and do not oscillate
particle or post-processing degradation path exists
no critical visual regression on desktop
items.abandonCondition: Abandon heuristic adaptation if it causes visible oscillation or unnecessarily reduces stable desktop quality; prefer deterministic device tiers or explicit fallback instead.
